dialog.nodeDesc: 【B】对峙阿尔玛 还原试用角色

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Thank you. It's a pleasure working with you as well.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: So their plan wasn't just to disrupt the Heliogyre by unleashing the Void Storm in Bjartr Woods. They also planned to...

items.speaker: Yngvar
items.text: A scientist... crippled... It's you.

items.speaker: Yngvar
items.text: All I have to do is get rid of you!

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I'm not really good at dealing with this kind of conversation.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Access to this lab, wearing Royan attire... You must be Yngvar, one of the successors to the Heliodic Six.

items.speaker: Yngvar
items.text: Heliodic Six. Ha, the Six.

items.speaker: Yngvar
items.text: Just because of my Resonance Ability would I be forced to spend my entire life trapped in that Heliogyre! They call it a Roya's highest honor, but did they ask if we wanted that fate?!

items.speaker: Yngvar
items.text: Everyone acts like they respect me, but the only one who truly wants to help me is that good lord from outside the tribe.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: From outside the tribe. You've joined forces with the Fractsidus?

items.speaker: Yngvar
items.text: Hah? What's that? Who cares?!

items.speaker: Yngvar
items.text: I just know that person promised that if I placed the device near the Solistree, my wish would come true.

items.speaker: Yngvar
items.text: Then I'd finally escape from this frozen wasteland and go start a new life in the New Federation!

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: You have no idea what you're doing, and no idea who you're aligning yourself with.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: This project proposal comes from the Spacetrek Collective, dated ten years ago. It outlines an idea to use the <te href=700010>Drive's</te> emergency response mechanism to artificially make it fall from the sky.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: And the method for triggering this mechanism is to pollute the Solistrees' root system with Voidmatter, making the Reactor Drive determine that the Threnodian had breached the lock.

items.speaker: Yngvar
items.text: You–you're saying that because I dropped that device there, the Drive is going to...

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Mornye, the Heliogyre is operational again. We can get the readings you need for the New Solar Ceremony.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: But something happening out there isn't right.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Yes. Once I've handled something here, I'll regroup with you.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Move aside. Before the Drive falls completely, I need to go back to launch Helios.

items.speaker: Yngvar
items.text: Hahaha... No, his orders were clear. You don't go anywhere. After that, no matter what happens to Lahai-Roi, I'm going to the New Federation to start my new life!

items.speaker: Yngvar
items.text: You're just a cripple. I won't let you stop me now!

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: It's been a while since I've heard someone talk like that. Since I had these legs, anyway.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I've been told that in situations like this...

items.speaker: Yngvar
items.text: Ah—wait—wait a second!

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I should make my displeasure very clear.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Alright... Now, it's about time I catch up with my mentor.

items.speaker: "The Shaper"
items.text: The Rite of Return, the ritual that once sealed the calamity deep underground.

items.speaker: "The Shaper"
items.text: Just now, the children have offered their strength.

items.speaker: "The Shaper"
items.text: The Heliogyre can once again begin its rotation.
Useless fool.
I told you to destroy the Heliogyre. Completely.

items.speaker: Yngvar
items.text: I'm sorry, but there's a reason for this! Some uninvited guests threw off the whole operation.
Save it. I don't want to hear your excuses.
At this point, our entire plan has fallen apart because you screwed up. And to think, I went out of my way to get a pass for you to the New Federation.

items.speaker: Yngvar
items.text: No! I'm begging you, don't leave me here! I'll do anything, just tell me what you need!
Ugh. You're lucky the one at the top gave someone as pathetic as you one last chance.
There's the other visitor in Bjartr Woods now, correct? Some crippled scientist.
Find her. Make sure she... doesn't make it back to the Collective.

items.speaker: Cody
items.text: Cause found! The Hyvatia Detector's signals are jammed!

items.speaker: Cody
items.text: Large-scale, highly concentrated Voidmatter detected on the surface. It's surpassing the measurement threshold. Calibration can't continue!

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: At a time like this...

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Adjust the measurement location. No, if the impact is this widespread, any adjustment would be pointless. Modify the detection components. No, even if theoretically feasible, there's no time to construct them.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: What about high-powered Voidmatter neutralizers? No, without the Drive's light, all energy components will be inoperable...

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: O-oh? Sorry, I'm just—

options.text: Tell me what's happening. We'll figure it out together.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: The launch failure is caused by the Detector's inability to measure the Drive's operating parameters. That's due to the high concentration of Voidmatter interfering with the readings.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: There are two potential solutions: We could increase the Detector's tolerance to Voidmatter. Or, we could eliminate the surrounding Voidmatter. But neither of those options are feasible in the time we have.

options.text: What if we transport it somewhere that isn't affected by Voidmatter?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Hmm, that's not realistic. The only places that aren't affected by Voidmatter right now are Bjartr Woods and Startorch Academy under the protective barrier. Or the Institute thousands of meters above...

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: High altitude.

options.text: Looks like there's another high altitude spot.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Do we have spares for Hyvatia's measurement modules?

items.speaker: Cody
items.text: Uh-yes! Researcher Anslem left behind several prototypes. I'll go fetch one from the warehouse.

items.speaker: Cody
items.text: But what do you intend to do with them?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: The eyes of the gods have failed us. So have our machines. Now, we move forward with our own hands.

dialog.stepLabel: Head to the Artificial Sun Lab

items.speaker: Warren
items.text: Where is Anslem at a time like this?! Who else can calibrate Hyvatia?

items.speaker: Warren
items.text: Cody, Vance, examine the orbital status of <te href=700017>Helios</te> again. Also, the propulsion system needs to be double-checked!

items.speaker: Cody
items.text: Professor, do you really plan to launch it without the raw data?

items.speaker: Warren
items.text: I understand your concerns... but we're on the verge of a disaster. The Collective can't just sit by and watch it happen.

items.speaker: Warren
items.text: This is my decision as the supervisor of this project. I will bear the consequences.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: No need for that.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: We've established contact with the Roya tribe and obtained the Drive's raw data. We can proceed with the New Solar Ceremony as originally planned.

items.speaker: Warren
items.text: ...Professor Mornye?

items.speaker: Warren
items.text: Did you just say you managed to obtain the data? Wonderful news. I'll prepare the connective instruments for you—

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: There's no need for us to continue pretending anymore.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Warren... Or should I say, traitor aligned with the Fractsidus.

items.speaker: Warren
items.text: That is a serious accusation, Professor Mornye.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I know. I meant it to be.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: You must be very familiar with this device, Warren.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: You handcrafted it to avoid leaving usage records... a clever idea.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: But since it is a "personal" creation... the marks left behind are like fingerprints.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: These are your marks. They've betrayed your deeds. Warren, you've desecrated the hard work of everyone involved.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Anslem's experiment accident, your current position in the project... It seems the Fractsidus played a part in both, didn't they?

items.speaker: Warren
items.text: Tch, that is a lot of assumptions and conjectures. With no evidence, I could easily sue you for slander—

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: You're right. All I have right now are assumptions and conjectures. But my ultimate goal isn't to have you face justice today.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Once we've identified the suspects, we'll have plenty of time to gather the evidence.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: What I need in this moment... is for you to step down as supervisor of this project.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: The one taking over... will be me.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: One more thing. Your subordinate provided us with some key evidence already.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: You want me to testify against Professor Warren? That's practically perjury… Sure, I could work some verbal magic, but honestly, I don't even know who I'm dealing with on the other side...

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: It is a dirty tactic, but it's effective. I'm confident in my analysis of those devices, but we'll need the university's verification to use them as evidence.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: In the limited time we have, the quickest way to detain Warren is your testimony.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Professor Mornye...

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: You can keep this recording. If I'm wrong, it'll be your guarantee later.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Or... you can refuse.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: —Nice working with you, then.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Thank you. It's a pleasure working with you as well.

items.speaker: Warren
items.text: (How did that traitor manage to...?!)

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I have already notified the Collective. The investigation will start soon.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: From here on out, {PlayerName} and I will be overseeing the launch procedure. All Spacetrek Collective personnel, to your stations!

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: {PlayerName}, please input the Drive's raw observational data into Helios.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Shaper, can you hear me?

items.speaker: "The Shaper"
items.text: Of course. To you have all the Heliogyre's readings been transmitted.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Then everything is ready. Let's commence with the New Solar Ceremony!
